questions.question: When entering a new social space, I usually...
options: A. Take note of every exit and blind spot before I can relax.
B. Find the person who looks most uncomfortable and talk to them.
C. Stand near a wall or solid structure and let the room come to me.
D. Drift between groups until something interesting catches me.
E. Immediately size up who seems to be in charge and why.

questions.question: Under physical exhaustion, I...
options: A. Get quieter and more focused until the job is done.
B. Find a second wind that feels almost angry.
C. Start making jokes or noise to keep morale up.
D. Slow down methodically; I won't break, but I won't rush either.
E. Feel my senses sharpen and my mind race ahead of my body.

questions.question: People who know me well would say my anger is...
options: A. Rare but volcanic; it changes the temperature of a room.
B. Ice-cold; I get very still and very precise.
C. Redirected into work, cleaning, or fixing something physical.
D. Expressed immediately and then genuinely forgotten.
E. Buried so deep they rarely see it, if ever.

questions.question: My favorite way to spend a completely free afternoon is...
options: A. Moving through the city or landscape without a plan.
B. Sitting in one spot and watching the world happen.
C. Working with my hands on something tangible and solid.
D. Being near water, even if I'm not doing anything in particular.
E. Losing myself in a crowd, concert, or event.

questions.question: When someone I care about is hurting, my first impulse is...
options: A. To physically put myself between them and the source of pain.
B. To say exactly what they need to hear, even if I have to invent the words.
C. To get them out of the environment entirely, no matter the cost.
D. To sit with them in silence until they're ready to talk.
E. To make them laugh or distract them with chaos.

questions.question: I am most uncomfortable when...
options: A. I am physically restrained or held in one place.
B. I have to perform emotions I do not actually feel.
C. There is no clear right answer or moral line to stand on.
D. I am the center of attention for longer than a few minutes.
E. Someone is in pain and I cannot identify the exact cause.

questions.question: In a crisis, I tend to...
options: A. Act before I think; my body moves on instinct.
B. Assess for a few seconds, then commit fully and completely.
C. Look for the weakest point in the problem and press there.
D. Gather the vulnerable and place myself in front of them.
E. Disappear from where I was and reappear where I'm most needed.

questions.question: I have been told I am...
options: A. Hard to read or figure out.
B. Intense or overwhelming at first meeting.
C. Reliable to the point of being predictable.
D. Always somewhere else mentally, even when present.
E. Warm but somehow distant at the same time.

questions.question: My relationship with my own body is...
options: A. A tool I trust completely; it does what I ask.
B. Something I feel disconnected from, like a vehicle I drive.
C. A source of intuition; my gut is always talking.
D. Flexible and forgiving; I push it and it adapts.
E. A battery that needs careful management and conservation.

questions.question: If I could change one thing about how I interact with the world, I would...
options: A. Be able to touch things without being seen.
B. Be able to reach farther than my arms allow.
C. Be able to carry more than I currently can.
D. Be able to feel what others feel before they speak.
E. Be able to leave without explanation or consequence.

questions.question: I sleep best when...
options: A. I am high up or near an open window.
B. I am enclosed and surrounded by walls on all sides.
C. I am exhausted to the point of emptiness.
D. There is white noise or constant movement nearby.
E. I feel emotionally safe with everyone in the house.

questions.question: My default response to a locked door is...
options: A. Find another way in.
B. Knock it down.
C. Pick the lock or find the key.
D. Wait for someone with a key.
E. Convince someone inside to open it.

questions.question: I am drawn to careers or roles that involve...
options: A. Protecting people who cannot protect themselves.
B. Fixing broken systems or broken bodies.
C. Observing and reporting without interfering.
D. Moving between worlds or groups as a connector.
E. Creating or destroying on a large scale.

questions.question: When I was a child, I often...
options: A. Climbed things and refused to come down.
B. Talked to animals or insects without embarrassment.
C. Broke things accidentally by being too rough or fast.
D. Hid and watched other children play.
E. Changed my personality to fit whoever was in the room.

questions.question: I trust...
options: A. What I can see with my own eyes.
B. What I can feel under my feet.
C. What I can sense in my chest about a person.
D. What I can build or fix with my hands.
E. What I can outlast.

questions.question: My ideal super-suit would be...
options: A. Something that looks normal until I need it.
B. Heavy and obvious, announcing my presence.
C. Light and barely there, like a second skin.
D. Covered in tools, gadgets, or adaptable elements.
E. Something that changes based on the mission.

questions.question: I am most energized by...
options: A. Storms, wind, or extreme weather.
B. Deep silence and solitude.
C. Physical competition or combat.
D. Emotional breakthroughs with other people.
E. Creating something from nothing.

questions.question: If I had to survive alone in the wilderness for a month, my advantage would be...
options: A. I can sleep anywhere and wake instantly alert.
B. I know how to find water and what plants won't kill me.
C. I can climb anything and see routes others miss.
D. I can stay so still that threats walk right past me.
E. I can sense weather changes before they arrive.

questions.question: I feel most like myself when...
options: A. I am moving through space without a destination.
B. I am rooted in one place, building or tending.
C. I am surrounded by people but not speaking.
D. I am changing someone's mind without them knowing I did it.
E. I am taking a hit and staying upright.

questions.question: Which of these recurring thoughts do you actually have?
options: A. 'I could have caught that if I'd been faster.'
B. 'I know exactly what they're thinking and I hate that I know.'
C. 'I wish I could just sink into the floor and disappear.'
D. 'Everything would be fine if they'd just let me handle it.'
E. 'This place would be better if I planted something here.'

questions.question: My social battery drains fastest when...
options: A. I have to perform emotions I don't feel.
B. I am physically trapped in a chair or room.
C. People unload trauma on me without asking.
D. There is no physical task to anchor me.
E. I have to explain myself repeatedly.

questions.question: I am most likely to be remembered for...
options: A. Being there when everything fell apart.
B. Saying the one thing that changed someone's life.
C. Getting people out of danger before they knew there was any.
D. Seeing what everyone else missed.
E. Adapting to whatever the moment required.

questions.question: If I could give one gift to everyone I love, it would be...
options: A. Skin that nothing could pierce.
B. The ability to know when someone is lying.
C. Wings to leave any place that harms them.
D. A second chance at anything they broke.
E. The strength to carry what they must.

questions.question: I am worst at...
options: A. Letting someone else take the lead.
B. Staying in one emotional state for long.
C. Pretending I don't know what I know.
D. Saying no when I'm stretched too thin.
E. Leaving a problem unsolved.

questions.question: Which of these would you actually do on instinct?
options: A. Catch a falling object without thinking.
B. Step between danger and someone smaller.
C. Mirror someone's accent or posture without meaning to.
D. Freeze completely when startled.
E. Reach toward an animal before reaching toward a person.

questions.question: I feel most powerless when...
options: A. I cannot move or escape.
B. I cannot understand why someone is hurting.
C. I cannot protect what I'm holding.
D. I cannot bend a rule or system to help someone.
E. I cannot warm up a cold room or cold heart.

questions.question: My dreams often involve...
options: A. Falling, flying, or floating.
B. Being unable to move while something approaches.
C. Speaking languages I don't know and being understood.
D. Burning or flooding a place that traps me.
E. Growing or stretching to reach someone far away.

questions.question: I recover from failure by...
options: A. Moving. New air, new view, new gravity.
B. Sitting in the dark until the tide turns.
C. Talking it through until it's just words.
D. Building or fixing something tangible.
E. Sleeping hard and waking up reset.

questions.question: I am most suspicious of...
options: A. People who are always cheerful.
B. People who never get angry.
C. People who need to be the fastest in every room.
D. People who can't sit with silence.
E. People who bend too easily.

questions.question: If I had to describe my willpower...
options: A. A flood that wears down stone.
B. A wall that stands until it crumbles completely.
C. A spark that catches or doesn't.
D. A current that finds every gap.
E. A net that holds many things at once.

questions.question: I am drawn to people who...
options: A. Need protection but won't ask.
B. Hide things and trust me to find them.
C. Move fast and take me with them.
D. Grow slowly into something beautiful.
E. Change shape to survive and let me watch.

questions.question: Which of these physical experiences do you actively enjoy?
options: A. The burn of pushing past your limit.
B. The chill of cold water or winter air.
C. The feeling of being submerged and weightless.
D. The pressure of a tight hug or heavy blanket.
E. The static shock of touching a doorknob.

questions.question: I would rather be...
options: A. Too strong to need help.
B. Too fast to be caught.
C. Too hidden to be hurt.
D. Too flexible to break.
E. Too aware to be surprised.

questions.question: If my life were a weather pattern, it would be...
options: A. A thunderstorm—sudden, loud, then clear.
B. A heavy fog—quiet, muffling, hiding everything.
C. A steady wind—constant, shaping, never still.
D. Deep earth—slow, warm, holding seeds.
E. A flash flood—overwhelming, then gone, leaving changed ground.

questions.question: The truth about me that few people know is...
options: A. I am always performing a version of myself.
B. I feel the weight of everyone I'm holding up.
C. I am terrified of being trapped more than being hurt.
D. I see connections between things that haven't happened yet.
E. I am more animal than they think.
